title: The Ashdown Affair
hook: A businessman is found dead after a private dinner, and the police cannot reconcile the witness timelines.
police_summary: The responding team built a competent first-pass file: the victim died after a private dinner at the Ashdown Hotel, three people have obvious motive or opportunity, and the timeline still refuses to settle into one coherent account. You have been called in because every witness statement seems clean until it is read against the others.

initial_open_questions: Who last saw Adrian Vale alive?
Why does the dinner timeline not match the hotel ledger?
What prompted the victim to leave the dining room early?

archive_domains.label: Crime Scene Packet
archive_domains.summary: Incident reports, witness statements, and corridor observations from the night of the death.

archive_domains.label: Medical and Forensics
archive_domains.summary: Autopsy extracts, toxicology notes, and physiological inconsistencies.

archive_domains.label: Financial Records
archive_domains.summary: Hotel ledgers, cash notes, and private bookings that never made the official first pass.

archive_domains.label: Communications and Logs
archive_domains.summary: Call summaries, drafts, and recovered communications metadata.

location_dossiers.label: Ashdown Hotel
location_dossiers.summary: The hotel where the dinner took place, including the west corridor, banquet hall, and the quietly booked suite linked to the case.

location_dossiers.label: Service Corridor
location_dossiers.summary: A narrow, polished staff corridor where Vale collapsed after leaving the dining room.

valid_board_links.notes: The hotel ledger suggests a private suite booking tied to the victim.
Call traffic implies Mara was hiding a business crisis.
